Vendor category

Fiat On and Off Ramp Providers for Web3

Discover providers that help users, wallets, exchanges, fintech apps and tokenization platforms move between bank money, cards, mobile money, stablecoins and digital assets.

Search ProvidersSubmit Provider
29Providers
6Service areas
170+Markets

Coverage

Explore fiat ramp service areas.

Use this category to compare embedded on-ramp widgets, off-ramp settlement, stablecoin infrastructure, crypto payment processing, banking rails and regional payment specialists.

01

Embeddable On-Ramp Widgets

Drop-in widgets and SDKs that let any app, wallet, or dApp embed fiat-to-crypto purchasing directly in their product. Users buy crypto without leaving the interface.

7 providers
02

Off-Ramp & Fiat Settlement

Convert crypto back to fiat currency and settle to bank accounts. Critical for businesses, payroll, merchant payments, and investors realizing gains.

5 providers
03

Stablecoin Infrastructure

Mint, redeem, and move stablecoins (USDC, USDT, PYUSD) at scale. The backbone of crypto-to-fiat and fiat-to-crypto flows for institutional and enterprise use.

5 providers
04

Crypto Payment Processing

Accept crypto payments from customers and settle in fiat or crypto. Card programs, invoicing, point-of-sale, and e-commerce integrations for merchants.

5 providers
05

Banking & Account Infrastructure

Banking-as-a-service for crypto companies: bank accounts, IBANs, virtual cards, and fiat rails integrated with digital asset platforms.

4 providers
06

Regional & Emerging Market Ramps

On/off-ramp providers specializing in markets where global providers have limited coverage: Latin America, Africa, Southeast Asia, and the Middle East.

4 providers

Directory

Search fiat on and off ramp providers.

Filter by provider name, region, payment method, service area, stablecoin capability or market focus.

AP

1 / Hybrid Crypto-Fiat Payment Gateway

Alchemy Pay

Best forMerchants and platforms in Asia-Pacific needing both crypto payment acceptance and fiat on/off-ramp services with local payment method support

Hybrid payment gateway supporting both crypto payments and fiat on/off-ramps with deep coverage of Asian payment methods. If your platform serves APAC users who pay with local methods (Alipay, GrabPay, GCash, local bank transfers), Alchemy Pay bridges those methods to crypto.

APAC Focus300+ Methods173 CountriesLocal Payments
Provider intelligence

Alchemy Pay provides a hybrid payment system that connects traditional payment methods to crypto. Their on-ramp supports 300+ fiat payment methods across 173 countries, with particular strength in Asia-Pacific: Alipay, WeChat Pay, GrabPay, GCash, local bank transfers, and mobile wallets. The off-ramp converts crypto back to local currencies. For crypto platforms, exchanges, and dApps targeting Asian markets, Alchemy Pay solves the local payment method problem. Global providers like MoonPay or Transak may support credit cards in these regions, but Alchemy Pay connects the local payment rails that users actually prefer.

HQ
Singapore
Founded
2018
Provider type
Hybrid Crypto-Fiat Payment Gateway
Service areas
Embeddable On-Ramp Widgets, Regional & Emerging Market Ramps
Coverage
APAC, Global
Visit alchemypay.org
BA

2 / Regulated On/Off-Ramp Infrastructure

Banxa

Best forExchanges and platforms needing a fully regulated on/off-ramp partner with licenses in multiple jurisdictions and white-label checkout

Regulated payment infrastructure for digital assets with licenses across multiple jurisdictions. If you need an on/off-ramp partner that holds its own regulatory licenses (not operating under exemptions), Banxa provides the compliance-first fiat gateway.

Publicly ListedMulti-LicensedWhite-Label100+ Countries
Provider intelligence

Banxa is a publicly listed (TSX: BNXA) fiat-to-crypto infrastructure provider with regulatory licenses in Australia, Europe, UK, USA, and Canada. Their white-label checkout supports credit/debit cards, bank transfers, Apple Pay, and local payment methods across 100+ countries. For platforms that need a regulated partner (not one operating in gray areas), Banxa's multi-jurisdiction licensing provides the compliance foundation. They handle KYC/AML, fraud prevention, and chargeback management. The white-label solution means users see your brand, not Banxa's.

HQ
Australia
Founded
2014
Provider type
Regulated On/Off-Ramp Infrastructure
Service areas
Embeddable On-Ramp Widgets, Off-Ramp & Fiat Settlement
Coverage
Australia, Europe, UK, USA, Canada
Visit banxa.com
BA

3 / Middle East & Africa On-Ramp

Beans App

Best forPlatforms serving users in the Middle East and Africa where global on-ramp providers have limited local payment method support

Fiat on-ramp specializing in the Middle East and Africa. If your users are in the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Nigeria, or other MENA/African markets and need to buy crypto using local payment methods (local bank transfers, mobile money), Beans fills the coverage gap.

MENAAfricaMobile MoneyLocal Banks
Provider intelligence

Global on-ramp providers often have limited support in MENA and African markets. Beans App specializes in these regions, supporting local bank transfers, mobile money (M-Pesa, MTN), and regional payment networks. They hold licenses and partnerships in UAE, Saudi Arabia, and key African markets. For crypto platforms targeting the fast-growing MENA and African crypto markets, Beans provides the local rails that MoonPay and Transak may not cover. The platform handles the regional KYC requirements, which vary significantly across these jurisdictions.

UAE

2020

BI

4 / Africa Bitcoin On/Off-Ramp

Bitnob

Best forBusinesses and individuals in Africa needing Bitcoin on/off-ramp services with instant local currency settlement via mobile money and bank transfers

Bitcoin-focused on/off-ramp for Africa. If your users are in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, or other African markets and need to buy, sell, or receive Bitcoin with instant settlement to local bank accounts or mobile money, Bitnob provides the Africa-first ramp.

Africa-FirstBitcoin FocusMobile MoneyInstant Settlement
Provider intelligence

Bitnob is built specifically for African markets where Bitcoin serves as both an investment and a practical tool for cross-border payments and savings. The platform supports: buying Bitcoin with Nigerian Naira, Ghanaian Cedi, Kenyan Shilling, and other local currencies via bank transfer and mobile money; selling Bitcoin to local currency with instant settlement; and cross-border payments using Bitcoin as the rail (send USD to a Nigerian recipient who receives Naira instantly). For crypto platforms, remittance companies, and businesses serving African users, Bitnob provides the local fiat rails with Bitcoin as the bridge.

Founded
2020

BI

5 / Crypto Payment Processor (Merchant)

BitPay

Best forEstablished businesses and merchants wanting to accept crypto payments from customers with automatic settlement to fiat (USD, EUR, GBP) in their bank account

The longest-running crypto payment processor for merchants. If you are a business that wants to accept Bitcoin and crypto payments from customers but receive US dollars (or euros, pounds) in your bank account the next business day, BitPay handles the conversion.

Since 2011Merchant FocusFiat SettlementMastercard
Provider intelligence

BitPay has been processing crypto payments for merchants since 2011. Their proposition is simple: customers pay in Bitcoin, Ethereum, or stablecoins, and the merchant receives fiat (USD, EUR, GBP, or others) settled to their bank account the next business day. Zero crypto volatility risk for the merchant. BitPay also provides: the BitPay Card (spend crypto anywhere Mastercard is accepted), invoicing tools, e-commerce plugins (Shopify, WooCommerce, BigCommerce), and a mobile app. For businesses that want crypto payment acceptance without holding crypto or managing volatility, BitPay is the established, battle-tested solution.

HQ
USA
Founded
2011
Provider type
Crypto Payment Processor (Merchant)
Service areas
Crypto Payment Processing, Off-Ramp & Fiat Settlement
Coverage
Global
Visit bitpay.com
BR

6 / Stablecoin API Platform

Bridge (by Stripe)

Best forDevelopers and fintechs needing API-first stablecoin orchestration: mint, transfer, and convert stablecoins across chains with simple REST APIs

Stablecoin API platform (acquired by Stripe). If you need programmatic stablecoin operations (mint USDC, convert between stablecoins, move across chains, settle to fiat) through clean REST APIs without building the infrastructure yourself, Bridge provides the developer-first stablecoin layer.

Stripe AcquiredAPI-FirstStablecoin OrchestrationDeveloper-Friendly
Provider intelligence

Bridge (acquired by Stripe in 2024) provides APIs for stablecoin orchestration. Their platform handles: converting fiat to stablecoins, converting between stablecoin types (USDC to USDT, PYUSD), moving stablecoins across blockchains, and settling stablecoins back to fiat in bank accounts. Bridge handles the regulatory complexity (money transmission licenses, KYC, sanctions screening) behind simple API calls. For fintech companies, neobanks, and digital asset platforms that want stablecoin functionality without building compliance infrastructure from scratch, Bridge provides the abstraction layer. The Stripe acquisition signals institutional confidence.

HQ
USA
Founded
2022
Provider type
Stablecoin API Platform
Service areas
Stablecoin Infrastructure, Off-Ramp & Fiat Settlement
Coverage
USA, Global
Visit bridge.xyz
CI

7 / USDC Issuer & Stablecoin Infrastructure

Circle (USDC)

Best forInstitutions, fintechs, and platforms needing the most regulated, transparent stablecoin (USDC) with enterprise-grade APIs for minting, redeeming, and transferring at scale

Issuer of USDC, the most regulated and transparent stablecoin. If your platform needs stablecoin infrastructure with full reserve backing (US Treasuries), regular attestations, and the most widely accepted institutional stablecoin, Circle provides USDC issuance, redemption, and cross-chain transfer at enterprise scale.

$30B+ USDCFull ReserveBig Four AttestedCCTP Cross-Chain
Provider intelligence

Circle issues USDC, a dollar-backed stablecoin with $30B+ in circulation, full reserve backing (US Treasuries and cash), and monthly attestations from a Big Four accounting firm. Circle's platform provides: USDC minting and redemption (convert dollars to USDC and back), Circle Mint (institutional on/off-ramp for USDC), Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol (CCTP, move USDC natively between 8+ blockchains), and programmable wallets. For tokenization platforms, USDC is often the settlement currency: investors deposit USDC to purchase tokenized assets, dividends are paid in USDC, and redemptions settle to USDC. Circle is the infrastructure layer for dollar-denominated digital asset operations.

HQ
USA
Founded
2013
Provider type
USDC Issuer & Stablecoin Infrastructure
Service areas
Stablecoin Infrastructure, Off-Ramp & Fiat Settlement
Coverage
Global
Visit circle.com

8 / Trusted Brand On-Ramp Widget

Coinbase On-Ramp

Best fordApps and wallets wanting to embed fiat-to-crypto purchasing backed by Coinbase's brand trust, compliance, and existing user base of 100M+ verified accounts

Embeddable on-ramp widget powered by Coinbase's infrastructure. If your dApp or wallet wants to offer fiat-to-crypto purchasing and you want the brand trust and compliance of Coinbase (100M+ verified accounts, publicly traded, US-regulated), their on-ramp widget drops into any interface.

Coinbase Trust100M+ UsersNASDAQ ListedZero Extra KYC
Provider intelligence

Coinbase On-Ramp provides an embeddable widget that lets users buy crypto directly within your application, powered by Coinbase's infrastructure. The key advantage is trust: Coinbase is the most recognized and trusted crypto brand in the US (publicly traded on NASDAQ, regulated). Users who already have Coinbase accounts can purchase with zero additional KYC. New users go through Coinbase's streamlined verification. The widget supports 100+ cryptocurrencies, credit/debit cards, bank transfers, and Apple Pay. For dApps targeting US users where conversion rate matters (users drop off at KYC), Coinbase's existing verified user base eliminates friction.

9 / Crypto-to-Fiat Settlement for Businesses

Conduit

Best forCrypto-native businesses needing to convert revenue from stablecoins/crypto to fiat for payroll, rent, vendor payments, and operational expenses

Business-focused crypto-to-fiat settlement. If your crypto company earns revenue in stablecoins or tokens but needs to pay employees, landlords, and vendors in dollars, Conduit converts your crypto treasury to fiat and handles the payments.

Business TreasuryPayrollVendor PaymentsAutomated
Provider intelligence

Conduit solves the daily operational problem of crypto-native businesses: you earn revenue in USDC, ETH, or other tokens, but your expenses are in fiat. Payroll, office rent, legal fees, SaaS subscriptions, and vendor invoices all need dollars. Conduit provides: automated conversion of crypto to fiat on a schedule, direct payment to employees' bank accounts (payroll), vendor payment management, tax reporting assistance, and treasury management tools. For DAOs, DeFi protocols, and crypto startups that hold treasury in stablecoins, Conduit provides the bridge from crypto treasury to fiat operations without manually selling on exchanges and wiring funds.

HQ
USA
Founded
2022
Provider type
Crypto-to-Fiat Settlement for Businesses
Service areas
Off-Ramp & Fiat Settlement, Banking & Account Infrastructure
Coverage
USA
Visit conduit.financial
KA

10 / Multi-Chain On/Off-Ramp

Kado

Best forDeFi protocols and dApps on non-Ethereum chains (Cosmos, Solana, Near, Osmosis) needing on/off-ramp widgets with native chain support

On/off-ramp with native support for non-Ethereum ecosystems. If your dApp is on Cosmos, Solana, Near, Osmosis, Injective, or other non-EVM chains and you need users to buy crypto directly on those chains (not buy on Ethereum and bridge), Kado provides native multi-chain ramps.

Non-EVM NativeCosmos/SolanaDirect ChainNo Bridging
Provider intelligence

Most on-ramp providers are Ethereum/EVM-first and treat alternative chains as secondary. Kado provides native support for non-EVM ecosystems: Cosmos (ATOM, OSMO directly to Osmosis), Solana (SOL, USDC on Solana), Near, Injective, Sei, and others. Users buy directly on their target chain without bridging. For DeFi protocols on Cosmos or Solana, this removes the UX nightmare of 'buy ETH on Ethereum, bridge to Cosmos, swap for OSMO.' Kado also supports off-ramp (sell crypto to bank account) on these chains. The embeddable widget integrates into any dApp.

HQ
USA
Founded
2022
Provider type
Multi-Chain On/Off-Ramp
Service areas
Embeddable On-Ramp Widgets, Off-Ramp & Fiat Settlement
Coverage
USA, Global
Visit kado.money

11 / Africa Mobile Money On/Off-Ramp

Kotani Pay

Best forCrypto platforms needing mobile money integration for African markets where users transact primarily through M-Pesa, MTN, Airtel Money, not bank accounts

Mobile money to crypto bridge for Africa. If your platform serves users in Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Ghana, or other African markets where mobile money (M-Pesa, MTN, Airtel Money) is the primary financial tool (not bank accounts), Kotani Pay provides the mobile money rails to crypto.

Mobile MoneyM-Pesa/MTNAfrica350M+ Users
Provider intelligence

In many African markets, more people have mobile money accounts than bank accounts. M-Pesa in Kenya processes more transactions than many national banking systems. Kotani Pay bridges mobile money to blockchain: users can buy crypto using M-Pesa, MTN Mobile Money, Airtel Money, and other mobile wallets, and sell crypto back to mobile money instantly. The platform provides APIs for integration into crypto platforms, DeFi protocols, and remittance services. For crypto projects targeting Africa's 350M+ mobile money users, Kotani Pay provides the rails that traditional on-ramp providers (which require bank accounts or credit cards) cannot.

LA

12 / Latin America On/Off-Ramp

Latamex

Best forCrypto platforms needing fiat on/off-ramps in Brazil, Argentina, Mexico, and other Latin American markets with local currency and payment method support

Fiat on/off-ramp for Latin America. If your platform serves users in Brazil (PIX, Boleto), Argentina (local bank transfers), Mexico (SPEI, OXXO), or other LATAM markets, Latamex provides the local payment rails that global providers often do not cover well.

LATAMPIX/BoletoSPEI/OXXOLocal Compliance
Provider intelligence

Latin America has some of the highest crypto adoption rates globally, but unique payment infrastructure. Brazil uses PIX (instant payments) and Boleto (cash vouchers). Argentina uses local bank transfers with currency controls. Mexico uses SPEI and OXXO (convenience store payments). Latamex provides on/off-ramp infrastructure that connects these local payment methods to crypto. For exchanges, wallets, and DeFi platforms targeting Latin American users, Latamex solves the local rails problem. They handle the region-specific compliance requirements (Brazilian CPF verification, Argentine currency controls, Mexican RFC requirements).

HQ
Argentina
Founded
2019
Provider type
Latin America On/Off-Ramp
Service areas
Regional & Emerging Market Ramps, Embeddable On-Ramp Widgets
Coverage
Brazil, Argentina, Mexico, Latin America

13 / Crypto Account Aggregation & Transfer

Mesh (formerly Front Finance)

Best forFintech apps needing to connect to users' existing crypto exchange accounts (Coinbase, Binance, Kraken) for portfolio viewing and asset transfers

Connect to users' existing crypto accounts. If your app needs to let users link their Coinbase, Binance, Kraken, or other exchange accounts to view balances, transfer assets, or initiate trades without leaving your interface, Mesh provides the account aggregation and transfer layer.

Plaid for Crypto300+ IntegrationsAccount LinkingAsset Transfer
Provider intelligence

Mesh (formerly Front Finance) provides APIs that connect to 300+ crypto exchanges, wallets, and brokerages. Users link their existing accounts (Coinbase, Binance, Kraken, Robinhood, MetaMask, etc.) through Mesh, and your app can: view their balances and positions, initiate transfers from their exchange to your platform, and execute trades on their behalf. This is Plaid for crypto. For digital asset platforms, Mesh solves onboarding: instead of asking users to manually transfer crypto (copy address, paste, confirm, wait), Mesh lets them move assets from their existing exchange in a few clicks. Reduces onboarding friction dramatically.

USA
Founded

Provider type

14 / European On/Off-Ramp & Crypto Banking

Mercuryo

Best forCrypto platforms serving European users needing on/off-ramp with SEPA support, virtual IBANs, and Euro-denominated crypto-fiat operations

European-focused crypto payment and on/off-ramp infrastructure. If your platform primarily serves European users and needs SEPA bank transfers, Euro-denominated accounts, virtual IBANs, and crypto card programs, Mercuryo provides the EU-regulated fiat gateway.

EU RegulatedSEPA InstantVirtual IBANEMI License
Provider intelligence

Mercuryo provides payment infrastructure with strong European coverage: SEPA instant transfers (settle Euros in minutes, not days), virtual IBANs for crypto companies, multi-currency wallets, and Visa/Mastercard crypto cards. They hold regulatory licenses in Europe (EMI license) and provide: on-ramp (buy crypto with SEPA, cards, Apple Pay), off-ramp (sell crypto to SEPA), and B2B services (crypto payroll, treasury management). For crypto platforms where European users are the primary market, Mercuryo's SEPA integration and EU regulatory positioning provide advantages over US-centric providers. They also support GBP (Faster Payments) and other currencies.

15 / Instant On-Ramp (No Pop-Up)

Meso

Best forWallets and dApps wanting the fastest possible on-ramp experience where returning users can buy crypto in seconds without pop-ups or redirects

Instant on-ramp that embeds natively (no pop-up windows, no redirects). If your wallet or dApp wants returning users to buy crypto in literally 3 seconds (card on file, no KYC repeat, no redirect), Meso provides the fastest embedded purchase experience.

No Pop-Up3-Second PurchaseNative EmbedSolana-First
Provider intelligence

Most on-ramp widgets open pop-up windows or redirect users to a third-party checkout page. Meso embeds natively: the purchase happens inside your app with no visual interruption. For returning users (card on file, KYC already completed), purchasing takes seconds. This matters because every pop-up, redirect, and loading screen reduces conversion. For wallets and dApps where the on-ramp is a core user flow (not just a nice-to-have), Meso's native embedding and instant purchase speed directly impact revenue. Currently focused on Solana ecosystem with expanding chain support.

USA
Founded

Provider type

16 / Premium Fiat On-Ramp (Consumer Brand)

MoonPay

Best forConsumer-facing crypto apps and NFT platforms wanting the most recognizable on-ramp brand with the broadest payment method and currency coverage

The most widely recognized consumer crypto on-ramp. If your app serves mainstream consumers (not just crypto natives) and you want an on-ramp that users may already know from buying NFTs, gaming tokens, or crypto elsewhere, MoonPay provides the strongest consumer brand recognition.

160+ CountriesStrongest BrandMetaMask/OpenSea80+ Methods
Provider intelligence

MoonPay is the most widely embedded on-ramp in consumer crypto: integrated into MetaMask, Bitcoin.com, OpenSea, and hundreds of wallets and dApps. Their coverage is extensive: 160+ countries, 30+ currencies, 80+ payment methods (credit/debit cards, bank transfers, Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay, PIX, iDEAL, and many local methods). MoonPay also provides: off-ramp (sell crypto to bank account), NFT checkout (buy NFTs with credit cards), and concierge service for high-net-worth buyers. For consumer-facing apps where brand recognition reduces friction (users trust a name they have seen before), MoonPay's widespread presence is the advantage.

USA/UK

Provider type

ON

17 / On-Ramp Aggregator

Onramper

Best forPlatforms wanting to offer users the best rate across multiple on-ramp providers (MoonPay, Transak, Banxa, etc.) through a single widget integration

Aggregates multiple on-ramp providers into a single widget. If you do not want to pick one on-ramp provider (each has different rates, coverage, and conversion rates), Onramper shows users the best option from MoonPay, Transak, Banxa, Mercuryo, and others in one interface.

AggregatorBest RatesMulti-ProviderOne Integration
Provider intelligence

Instead of integrating a single on-ramp provider (and being locked into their rates and coverage), Onramper aggregates multiple providers and shows users the best option for their specific transaction. A user in Germany buying ETH with SEPA might get the best rate from Mercuryo, while a user in Brazil using PIX might get better rates from another provider. Onramper handles this comparison automatically. For platforms, this means: one integration instead of many, always-best rates for users, broader geographic coverage (if one provider does not cover a country, another might), and higher conversion rates (more payment options = more users can complete purchases).

HQ
Netherlands
Founded
2020
Provider type
On-Ramp Aggregator
Service areas
Embeddable On-Ramp Widgets
Coverage
Global
Visit onramper.com

18 / Mass-Market Crypto On/Off-Ramp & Stablecoin

PayPal (PYUSD)

Best forPlatforms wanting to reach PayPal's 430M+ user base for crypto on/off-ramp, plus PYUSD stablecoin integration for payments and settlement

PayPal's crypto services and PYUSD stablecoin. If you want to reach the largest existing user base for fiat-to-crypto conversion (430M+ PayPal accounts, already KYC'd, payment methods on file), PayPal provides the most frictionless entry point for mainstream users.

430M+ UsersPYUSD StablecoinZero FrictionMainstream
Provider intelligence

PayPal offers crypto buying/selling (Bitcoin, Ethereum, Litecoin, Bitcoin Cash, PYUSD) to its 430M+ users, and issues PYUSD, a dollar-backed stablecoin on Ethereum and Solana. The advantage is massive: PayPal users are already verified, have payment methods on file, and trust the brand. For platforms that integrate PayPal checkout, adding crypto purchasing requires minimal additional friction. PYUSD provides: a stablecoin backed by PayPal's regulatory infrastructure, seamless conversion between PayPal balance and PYUSD, and growing DeFi integration. For tokenization platforms targeting mainstream (non-crypto-native) investors, PayPal may be the lowest-friction entry point.

19 / Developer-First On/Off-Ramp SDK

Ramp Network

Best forDevelopment teams wanting the most customizable on-ramp SDK with deep integration options, webhook events, and white-label capabilities

Developer-first on/off-ramp SDK with deep customization. If your engineering team wants an on-ramp they can deeply integrate (custom UI, webhook events, programmatic control, white-label branding) rather than just dropping in a generic widget, Ramp provides the developer-focused toolkit.

Developer SDKDeep CustomizationWebhooksWhite-Label
Provider intelligence

Ramp Network provides an on-ramp SDK designed for developers who want control. Unlike generic widgets, Ramp offers: deep UI customization (match your brand completely), webhook events (get notified when purchases complete, fail, or require action), programmatic flow control (pre-fill amounts, lock currencies, skip steps), and white-label capabilities. The SDK supports 150+ countries, bank transfers, cards, Apple Pay, and Google Pay. For platforms where the on-ramp is a critical user flow (not just an afterthought) and the engineering team wants to optimize conversion through custom integration, Ramp provides the developer tools that generic widgets lack.

UK/Poland
Founded
2017
Provider type

Coverage

SA

20 / Fraud Prevention & Instant Settlement

Sardine

Best forOn-ramp providers and exchanges needing AI-powered fraud detection that enables instant settlement (release crypto immediately instead of waiting 3-5 days for ACH)

AI fraud prevention that enables instant crypto purchases. If your platform wants to release crypto to users immediately when they pay by bank transfer (instead of waiting 3-5 days for ACH settlement), Sardine's fraud AI assesses risk in real-time and enables instant delivery.

Instant SettlementAI FraudBehavioral BiometricsRisk Engine
Provider intelligence

The biggest friction in fiat on-ramps: users pay by bank transfer (ACH), but the platform must wait 3-5 days for settlement before releasing crypto. During that time, the user cannot use their purchase. Sardine solves this with AI fraud detection that assesses each transaction in real-time. If the fraud risk is low (Sardine's AI checks device fingerprint, behavioral biometrics, account history, and hundreds of other signals), the platform can release crypto instantly, before the bank transfer settles. Sardine assumes the fraud risk. For on-ramp providers and exchanges, this transforms the user experience: instant delivery instead of multi-day holds.

HQ
USA
Founded
2020
Provider type
Fraud Prevention & Instant Settlement
Service areas
Banking & Account Infrastructure, Embeddable On-Ramp Widgets
Coverage
USA, Global
Visit sardine.ai
SP

21 / Decentralized Payment Protocol (Solana)

Solana Pay

Best forMerchants and apps on Solana wanting free, instant, direct crypto payments without intermediaries using QR codes and deep links

Open payment protocol on Solana for direct, fee-less transactions. If you want your customers to pay with USDC, SOL, or any Solana token directly to your wallet with zero processing fees (no intermediary takes a cut), Solana Pay provides the peer-to-peer payment protocol.

Zero FeesInstantOpen ProtocolShopify Plugin
Provider intelligence

Solana Pay is an open protocol, not a company. It enables direct wallet-to-wallet payments on Solana using QR codes and deep links. Key difference from traditional payment processors: there is no intermediary. The customer scans a QR code, confirms in their wallet, and the merchant receives the payment directly. Transaction fees are fractions of a penny (Solana's base fees). No chargebacks, no processing fees, no 3-5 day settlement. For merchants with crypto-native customers, Solana Pay eliminates the 2.5-3% credit card processing fees entirely. Integrated into Shopify through the Solana Pay plugin. The protocol supports transaction requests for programmable payment flows.

HQ
Global
Founded
2022
Provider type
Decentralized Payment Protocol (Solana)
Service areas
Crypto Payment Processing
Coverage
Global
Visit solanapay.com
SP

22 / Stablecoin Payment & Invoicing

Sphere

Best forBusinesses wanting to accept stablecoin payments (USDC, USDT) with invoicing, recurring billing, and automatic fiat conversion built for B2B and SaaS

Stablecoin payment infrastructure for businesses. If you want to invoice clients in USDC, set up recurring stablecoin billing, or accept stablecoins on your website with optional auto-conversion to fiat, Sphere provides the business payment stack for stablecoins.

B2B StablecoinsInvoicingRecurring BillingAuto-Convert
Provider intelligence

Sphere provides payment infrastructure specifically for stablecoin-denominated business operations: create and send invoices payable in USDC/USDT, set up recurring billing (SaaS subscriptions, retainers), embed checkout widgets on websites, and auto-convert received stablecoins to fiat. For B2B companies in the crypto industry (agencies billing crypto clients, SaaS tools serving DAOs, service providers working with protocols), Sphere replaces the manual process of 'send me USDC to this address' with professional invoicing, payment tracking, and reconciliation. Automatic fiat conversion means the business receives dollars while the client pays in stablecoins.

USA
Founded
2022
Provider type

Coverage

23 / Stablecoin-to-Fiat Settlement

STRIDE

Best forInstitutional platforms and OTC desks needing high-volume stablecoin-to-fiat conversion with compliance infrastructure and banking partnerships

Institutional stablecoin-to-fiat settlement infrastructure. If your platform processes large stablecoin volumes and needs reliable, compliant conversion to fiat with banking partnerships, STRIDE provides the institutional-grade settlement layer.

InstitutionalHigh VolumeBanking PartnersCompliance
Provider intelligence

STRIDE provides stablecoin-to-fiat settlement infrastructure for institutional volumes. Their platform connects to banking partners for reliable fiat conversion, handles compliance requirements (KYB, transaction monitoring, sanctions screening), and supports high-throughput processing. For tokenization platforms, OTC desks, and institutional trading venues where stablecoin settlement volumes are significant, STRIDE provides the reliable off-ramp that does not break at scale. Consumer on-ramps handle retail volumes. STRIDE handles institutional flows where reliability and compliance matter more than consumer UX.

HQ
USA
Founded
2022
Provider type
Stablecoin-to-Fiat Settlement
Service areas
Stablecoin Infrastructure, Off-Ramp & Fiat Settlement
Coverage
USA, Global
Visit strideup.io
SC

24 / Enterprise Fiat-to-Crypto Rails

Stripe Crypto

Best forEstablished businesses with existing Stripe integration wanting to add crypto on-ramp, stablecoin payouts, and crypto payment acceptance to their existing payment stack

Stripe's crypto and stablecoin products. If your business already uses Stripe for payments and wants to add crypto capabilities (on-ramp for users, stablecoin payouts to creators/sellers, crypto payment acceptance) within your existing Stripe integration, Stripe provides the seamless extension.

$1T+ VolumeExisting IntegrationStablecoin PayoutsEnterprise
Provider intelligence

Stripe, the dominant internet payment processor ($1T+ in annual volume), now offers crypto products: fiat-to-crypto on-ramp (embedded widget for buying crypto), stablecoin payouts (pay creators, sellers, or contractors in USDC instead of waiting for international wire transfers), and crypto payment acceptance (merchants accept Bitcoin and stablecoins through Stripe Checkout). The advantage for existing Stripe merchants is integration: adding crypto requires minimal additional development on top of existing Stripe integration. For businesses with global payees (marketplaces, creator platforms, gig economy), stablecoin payouts via Stripe settle instantly instead of 3-5 day international wires. The Bridge acquisition strengthened Stripe's stablecoin infrastructure.

HQ
USA
Founded
2010
Provider type
Enterprise Fiat-to-Crypto Rails
Service areas
Crypto Payment Processing, Stablecoin Infrastructure
Coverage
Global
Visit stripe.com
TE

25 / Largest Stablecoin Issuer

Tether (USDT)

Best forPlatforms needing the most liquid and widely traded stablecoin (USDT) with the deepest trading pairs across centralized and decentralized exchanges globally

Issuer of USDT, the largest stablecoin by market cap ($110B+) and the most traded cryptocurrency by volume. If your platform needs the stablecoin with the deepest liquidity, the most trading pairs, and the widest exchange support globally, USDT is the default.

$110B+Most LiquidEvery ExchangeMost Traded
Provider intelligence

Tether issues USDT, the most widely used stablecoin with $110B+ in circulation and more daily trading volume than Bitcoin. USDT is available on virtually every blockchain (Ethereum, Tron, Solana, Avalanche, Polygon, and many others) and every centralized exchange. For trading platforms, USDT/token pairs are the most liquid. For cross-border payments, USDT on Tron is the de facto standard for remittances in emerging markets. For tokenization platforms, USDT provides the liquidity advantage: more users hold USDT than any other stablecoin. The trade-off vs USDC: USDT has more liquidity but less transparency (no Big Four attestations). Most platforms support both.

HQ
British Virgin Islands
Founded
2014
Provider type
Largest Stablecoin Issuer
Service areas
Stablecoin Infrastructure
Coverage
Global
Visit tether.to
TR

26 / Global On/Off-Ramp (Widest Coverage)

Transak

Best forCrypto platforms needing the widest country and payment method coverage in a single on-ramp provider, especially for non-US markets

On/off-ramp with the widest global coverage: 170+ countries, 75+ payment methods, 130+ cryptocurrencies. If you need a single on-ramp provider that covers the most countries and local payment methods (especially in markets where other providers are weak), Transak provides the broadest reach.

170+ Countries75+ Methods130+ CryptosWidest Coverage
Provider intelligence

Transak differentiates on coverage breadth: 170+ countries, 75+ payment methods, 130+ cryptocurrencies, and support for non-EVM chains. While MoonPay has the strongest brand and Ramp has the best developer tools, Transak covers more edge cases: more countries, more local payment methods, more chains. For platforms with a truly global user base where users come from Indonesia, Turkey, Vietnam, Colombia, and other markets that tier-1 providers may not fully cover, Transak's breadth ensures fewer users hit 'not available in your country.' They also provide off-ramp to bank accounts and crypto-to-crypto conversion.

HQ
USA/India
Founded
2019
Provider type
Global On/Off-Ramp (Widest Coverage)
Service areas
Embeddable On-Ramp Widgets, Off-Ramp & Fiat Settlement
Coverage
Global
Visit transak.com
UN

27 / Global Payment Platform with Crypto

Unlimit (formerly Payneteasy)

Best forEnterprises needing a single payment platform that handles both traditional payments (cards, bank transfers) and crypto on/off-ramp across 170+ countries

Global payment platform offering traditional payments and crypto capabilities in one platform. If your business needs both fiat payment processing (cards, bank transfers, local methods) and crypto on/off-ramp under a single provider with unified reporting, Unlimit provides the converged payment stack.

Unified PlatformFiat + Crypto170+ CountriesSingle Integration
Provider intelligence

Unlimit (formerly Payneteasy) is a global payment platform that serves both traditional and crypto payment needs. Their crypto vertical provides on-ramp (buy crypto via cards and bank transfers), off-ramp (sell crypto to bank accounts), and crypto-to-fiat settlement, all integrated with their traditional payment processing. The advantage: one platform, one integration, one compliance framework for both fiat and crypto operations. For businesses transitioning from pure fiat to hybrid fiat-crypto operations (marketplaces adding crypto payouts, e-commerce adding crypto payments), Unlimit eliminates the need to integrate separate providers for fiat and crypto.

Visit unlimit.com
WY

28 / Crypto Compliance & Transfer API

Wyre (Legacy/API)

Best forPlatforms with existing Wyre integration or developers needing compliant crypto transfer APIs with built-in AML screening for programmatic fiat-to-crypto operations

Compliance-focused crypto transfer API. Wyre provides programmatic fiat-to-crypto conversion with built-in AML/KYC compliance. For platforms that need API-level control over fiat-to-crypto flows with compliance baked in (not bolted on), Wyre provides the regulated transfer infrastructure.

Compliance-FirstAPI TransferAML Built-InProgrammatic
Provider intelligence

Wyre provides API infrastructure for programmatic crypto operations with compliance built into every transaction. Their APIs handle: fiat-to-crypto conversion, crypto-to-fiat conversion, cross-border transfers, and wallet management, all with integrated KYC/AML screening, sanctions checks, and transaction monitoring. For fintech platforms and payment companies that need to move money between fiat and crypto programmatically (not through consumer widgets), Wyre provides the compliance-first API layer. Note: Wyre has gone through operational changes. Verify current availability and service status before integration.

Service areas

Visit sendwyre.com
YC

29 / Africa's Largest Crypto On/Off-Ramp

Yellow Card

Best forCrypto platforms and remittance companies needing the widest African coverage with support for 20+ African countries, local currencies, and mobile money

The largest crypto on/off-ramp in Africa with coverage across 20+ countries. If your platform serves African users across multiple countries and needs a single provider covering Nigeria, South Africa, Kenya, Ghana, Tanzania, and beyond, Yellow Card provides the widest African reach.

20+ African CountriesMost LicensedMulti-CurrencyMobile Money
Provider intelligence

Yellow Card is the most broadly licensed crypto company in Africa, operating across 20+ countries with support for local currencies and payment methods in each. They handle: buying crypto with Nigerian Naira, South African Rand, Kenyan Shilling, Ghanaian Cedi, and other local currencies; selling crypto back to local currency; and mobile money integration across the continent. For international remittance companies using crypto rails, Yellow Card provides the African off-ramp (send USDC, recipient receives local currency). For exchanges and platforms, Yellow Card provides the compliance infrastructure (licenses across multiple African jurisdictions) that is extremely difficult to build independently.

HQ
USA/Nigeria
Founded
2016
Provider type
Africa's Largest Crypto On/Off-Ramp
Service areas
Regional & Emerging Market Ramps, Off-Ramp & Fiat Settlement
Coverage
Nigeria, South Africa, Kenya, Ghana, Tanzania, 20+ African Countries
Visit yellowcard.io

Selection framework

How to choose a ramp provider.

Ramp quality is not only about fees. Coverage, payment methods, approval rates, licensing, user experience and fraud controls shape the real conversion rate.

01

Start with the user geography

Confirm target countries, local payment methods, payout rails and any unsupported regions before comparing pricing.

02

Separate buy flows from sell flows

Some providers are strong for on-ramp but limited for off-ramp. Check bank payouts, stablecoin support and settlement timing.

03

Test KYC and payment conversion

Drop-off, card declines, fraud holds and repeated verification steps can matter more than the advertised fee.

04

Review compliance ownership

Clarify who handles KYC, AML, sanctions, fraud, chargebacks, licensing, support and reporting in each market.

FAQ

Fiat ramp provider questions.

What is the best fiat on-ramp for a Web3 app?

The best provider depends on user geography, payment methods, chain support, KYC flow and whether you need buy-only or both buy and sell flows. Many teams compare MoonPay, Transak, Ramp Network, Coinbase On-Ramp and Onramper first, then add regional specialists where conversion matters.

What is the difference between an on-ramp and an off-ramp?

A fiat on-ramp lets users buy crypto or stablecoins with bank transfers, cards or local payment methods. An off-ramp lets users sell crypto or stablecoins and receive fiat in a bank account, card, mobile money wallet or local payment rail.

Should a platform use one provider or an aggregator?

A single provider is simpler to launch and manage. An aggregator can improve coverage, pricing and conversion by routing users across multiple providers. Mature products often use both: direct integrations for key markets and an aggregator for long-tail coverage.

Which providers help with stablecoin settlement?

Circle, Tether, Bridge, Stripe Crypto, PayPal PYUSD, Sphere and STRIDE are examples of providers or infrastructure layers connected to stablecoin issuance, orchestration, settlement, payments or treasury workflows.

What should institutions check before choosing a ramp provider?

Review licensing posture, country coverage, supported payment methods, KYC and KYB responsibilities, fraud controls, chargeback handling, settlement timing, supported assets, blockchain networks, API quality and operational support.
